content_en: Prophet Yoosuf, peace be upon him, brought his blood brother close to himself and gave each two of his other brothers (from a different mother) a room; he gave his blood brother a room to himself, and said to him, as Allaah says in the sixty-ninth verse: п»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ пәҮЩҗп»Ёпұўп»І пәғЩҺп»Ёп№¶пәҺ пәғЩҺпәЁп№ёп»ӯп»ҷ п» п№ёп»ӯп»Ҙп»Өп»ҢпәҺ п»ңп№¶пәҺп»Ёп№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»ҙ пә’Щҗп»Өпәҳп№¶пәҢЩҗпәұп»“п№¶п»ј пәҳп№¶пә’ which means: "Indeed, I am your brother so do not despair over what they used to do [ie., to me]." [Yoosuf: 69] l Prophet Yoosuf, peace be upon him, used lawful trickery to keep his brother. Allaah says in the seventy and seventy-first verses: пәҺпәӯЩҗп»—п№ёп»ӯп»Ҙ п»ҹп№¶пәҙ пәҮЩҗп»Ёпұ п»ңп№ёп»ЎпәҺ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫп»ҢЩҗп»ҙпәӯпәҳп№ёп»¬ пәғЩҺп»ҙпә…ЩҺпә«пұўп»Ҙ п»Ө пәғЩҺпә«пұ п»ҘЩ„Щҗ пәғЩҺпәЁЩҗп»ҙп»ӘЩҗ пәңп№ёп»ЎпәӨпә”п№¶ п»“Щҗп»І пәӯп»ҳп№¶пәҺп»ҙЩ„ЩҺ пәҚп»ҹпәҙп»Ң пә пәҺпәҜЩҗп»«Щҗп»Ўп»¬ пә’Щҗпә п»Ўп»«пәҜп»¬пәҺ пә п»“п№¶п» п№¶п»Ө . пәҺпә«п№¶пәҚ п»Өп»¬Щҗп»Ўп» п№¶п»ҙп» п№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»ӢпәғЩҺп»—п№ҫпә’п»—п№¶пәҺп»ҹп№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»ӯ п»ӯп»Ҙпәҳп№¶п»”п№ҫп»ҳЩҗпә© which means: "So when he [i.e. Yoosuf] had furnished them with their supplies, he put the [gold measuring] bowl into the bag of his brother. Then an announcer called out, 'O caravan, indeed you are thieves'. They [i.e., the brothers] said while approaching them, 'What is it you are missing?'" [Yoosuf: 70-71] l It is permissible for one to give a fee in return for someone finding a lost item for him, or to hire someone to carry out a task, even if the task is unspecified - as long as the fee is specified. Allaah says in the seventy-second verse: ЩҚп»ҢЩҗп»ҙпәӯЩ„ЩҸ пә’ пә’Щҗп»ӘЩҗ пәӨЩҗп»ӨпәҺпәҖ пә п»Ҙп»ҹЩҗп»Өп» Щҗп»ҷЩҗ п»ӯ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫп»ӨпәҚп»үп»ӯ пәјп»—п№¶пәҺп»ҹп№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»Ёп№¶п»”п№ҫп»ҳЩҗпә© which means: "They [i.e., the workers of Yoosuf] said, 'We are missing the measure of the king. And for he who produces it is [the reward of] a camel's load.'" [Yoosuf: 72] l After this, Yoosuf, peace be upon him, progressively led his brothers into a trap by asking, as mentioned in the seventy-fourth verse: пәҚпә…ЩҸп»©пәҜпәҺ пә п»—п№¶пәҺп»ҹп№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»“п№¶п»Ө which means: "Then what would be its recompense [i.e., the punishment for theft]?" [Yoosuf: 74] So he led them to state the punishment themselves, which was at that time enslavement of the thief to the owner of what was stolen, which consequently enabled Yoosuf, peace be upon him, to keep his brother with him. His brothers replied, as Allaah says in the seventy-fifth verse: пәҚпә…ЩҸп»©пәҜ пә п»ӯп» Щҗп»ӘЩҗ п»“п№¶п»¬пәӨ п»“Щҗп»І пәӯпә Щҗпә© п»ӯп»Ҙ п»ӨпәҚпә…ЩҸп»©пәҜп»—п№¶пәҺп»ҹп№ёп»ӯпәҚ пә  which means: "'Its recompense is that he in whose bag it is found - he [himself] will be its recompense [a convicted thief was made a slave of the one from whom he had stolen].'" [Yoosuf: 75] l When one wishes to do something, he must exert all means and efforts to make it successful. Allaah says in the seventy-sixth verse: пәҺпәҖЩҗ пәғЩҺпәЁЩҗп»ҙп»Ә п»ӯЩҗп»ӢпәҺп»ӨпәҺ п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙп»¬пә пәҳп№¶пәЁп№ҫпәӯ пәҚпәҙпәҺпәҖЩҗ пәғЩҺпәЁЩҗп»ҙп»ӘЩҗ пәңп№ёп»ЎЩ„ЩҺ п»ӯЩҗп»Ӣ п»—п№¶пә’пәҳЩҗп»¬Щҗп»Ўп»ӢЩҗп»ҙпәғЩҺ пә’Щҗпә„ЩҺп»ӯпә©п»“п№¶пә’ which means: "So he began [the search] with their bags before the bag of his brother; then he extracted it from the bag of his brother." [Yoosuf: 76] Had he started with his blood brother's bag, his trick may have been exposed and discovered, so instead he started with the other brothers and then searched his blood brother's. Allaah had decreed that he would keep his brother, so He facilitated the matter for him without leaving any room for doubt from his other brothers. l Honouring pledges is extremely important in Islaam, and a Muslim must have a sense of responsibility and try his utmost to fulfil whatever he pledges to do. When the brothers gave up on retrieving their accused brother from Yoosuf, peace be upon him, they conducted a meeting as Allaah says in the eightieth verse: Щҗ пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»ӘпәңЩҗп»ҳпәҺп№° п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙп»ӯ п»Өп»ңп№ёп»Ўп» п№¶п»ҙ пәғЩҺпәЁп№¶пә«п№¶ п»Ӣ п»—п№¶пә©пәҺп»ңп№ёп»Ў пәғЩҺпә’п»ӯпәҚ пәғЩҺп»Ҙп» п№¶п»Ө пәҳп№¶п»Ң пәғЩҺп»ҹп№¶п»Ўп»Ўп»«пәҺ п»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ п»ңп№¶пә’Щҗп»ҙпәӯп»ӯпәҚ п»Ёп№¶пә Щҗп»ҙ пәЁп№¶п» п№¶пәјп»ӯпәҚ п»ӨЩҗп»Ёп№ҫп»Әпә„ЩҺпәҙпәҳп№¶п»ҙпәҺ пәҚпәҙп»“п№¶п» п№¶п»Ө which means: "So when they had despaired of him, they secluded themselves in private consultation. The eldest of them said, 'Do you not know that your father has taken upon you an oath by AllaahвҖҰ?'" [Yoosuf: 80] The eldest brother felt the responsibility of the pledge that they had given to their father and refused to leave until their father gave them all his permission to return without their younger brother, or until they found a way to solve the problem with the accused brother. This reflects that the brothers had changed from the time they had thrown Yoosuf, peace be upon him, in the well, and that they had repented to Allaah. l One must provide evidences to prove what he claims if he feels that people do not believe him, as Yoosuf's brothers did to their father, as Allaah says in the eighty-second verse: пәҺпә©Щҗп»—п№ёп»ӯп»ҘпәҮЩҗп»Ёпұ пәҺ п»ҹп№¶пәјпәҺ п»ӯп» п№ҫп»Ёп№¶пәҺ п»“Щҗп»ҙп»¬ пәҚп»ҹпұ пәҳЩҗп»І пәғЩҺп»—п№ҫпә’пәҚп»ҹп№ҫп»ҢЩҗп»ҙпәӯпәҺ п»ӯпә”п№¶ пәҚп»ҹпұ пәҳЩҗп»І п»ңп№ёп»Ёпұ пәҺ п»“Щҗп»ҙп»¬п»ҙпә„ЩҺЩ„Щҗ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫп»ҳп№¶пәӯпәҚпәҙп»ӯ which means: "And ask the city in which we were and the caravan in which we came - and indeed, we are truthful." [Yoosuf: 82] Meaning: ask other sources to confirm our story. l One must always think good of Allaah as this is one of the implications of Islaamic Monotheism. Despite Ya'qoob, peace be upon him, being away from his son Yoosuf, peace be upon him, for more than ten years, he said as Allaah says in the eighty-third verse: п»ӨЩҗп»ҙп»ҢпәҺп№° пә п»ЁЩҗп»І пә’Щҗп»¬Щҗп»Ўпә„Щ’пәҳЩҗп»ҙ п»ҙ пәғЩҺп»Ҙп»° пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Әпәҙп»Ӣ which means: "Perhaps Allaah will bring them [i.e., his sons] to me all together." [Yoosuf: 83] l Crying does not contradict having patience. Allaah says about prophet Ya'qoob, peace be upon him, in the eighty-fourth verse: Щҗп»ҘпәҜ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫпәӨ п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙп»Ёп№¶пәҺп»©п»ҙпә•п№ҫ п»Ӣп»Җп»ҙпәҚпә’п»‘п№¶ п»ӯп»ӯпәҙп» п№¶п»° п»ҙп»”п№¶п»° п»ӢпәҺ пәғЩҺпәҙп»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ п»ҙп»ӯ which means: "And he turned away from them and said, 'O my sorrow over Yoosuf!' and his eyes became white [i.e.
content_id: Nabi Yusuf, alaihissalam, mendekatkan saudara kandungnya kepada dirinya dan memberikan kamar kepada masing-masing dua saudara-saudaranya yang lain (dari ibu yang berbeda); ia memberikan kamar tersendiri kepada saudara kandungnya, dan berkata kepadanya sebagaimana Allah berfirman dalam ayat keenam puluh sembilan yang artinya: "Sesungguhnya aku ini adalah saudaramu, maka janganlah kamu berduka cita terhadap apa yang telah mereka kerjakan." [Yusuf: 69] l Nabi Yusuf, alaihissalam, menggunakan tipu daya yang halal untuk menahan saudaranya. Allah berfirman dalam ayat ketujuh puluh dan ketujuh puluh satu yang artinya: "Maka tatkala telah disiapkan untuk mereka bahan makanan mereka, Yusuf memasukkan piala (tempat minum) ke dalam karung saudaranya. Kemudian berteriaklah seseorang yang menyerukan: Hai kafilah, sesungguhnya kamu adalah orang-orang yang mencuri. Mereka menjawab sambil menghadap kepada penyeru itu: Barang apakah yang hilang dari kamu?" [Yusuf: 70-71] l Diperbolehkan memberikan upah sebagai imbalan atas seseorang yang menemukan barang yang hilang, atau menyewa seseorang untuk melaksanakan tugas, meskipun tugasnya tidak dirinci, selama upahnya sudah ditentukan. Allah berfirman dalam ayat ketujuh puluh dua yang artinya: "Mereka menjawab: Kami kehilangan piala raja, dan siapa yang dapat mengembalikannya akan memperoleh bahan makanan (seberat) beban unta, dan aku menjamin terhadapnya." [Yusuf: 72] l Setelah itu, Yusuf, alaihissalam, secara bertahap menggiring saudara-saudaranya ke dalam perangkap dengan bertanya, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam ayat ketujuh puluh empat yang artinya: "Mereka bertanya: Apa hukumannya?" [Yusuf: 74] Maka ia mengarahkan mereka untuk menyatakan hukumannya sendiri, yang pada saat itu adalah perbudakan pencuri kepada pemilik barang yang dicuri, yang akibatnya memungkinkan Yusuf, alaihissalam, untuk menahan saudaranya bersamanya. Saudara-saudaranya menjawab, sebagaimana Allah berfirman dalam ayat ketujuh puluh lima yang artinya: "Mereka menjawab: Hukumannya ialah pada siapa diketemukan (barang yang hilang) dalam kantongnya, maka dia sendirilah sebagai hukumannya." [Yusuf: 75] l Ketika seseorang ingin melakukan sesuatu, ia harus mengerahkan semua cara dan upaya agar berhasil. Allah berfirman dalam ayat ketujuh puluh enam yang artinya: "Maka mulailah Yusuf (memeriksa) karung-karung mereka sebelum (memeriksa) karung saudaranya sendiri, kemudian dia mengeluarkan piala raja itu dari karung saudaranya." [Yusuf: 76] Seandainya ia mulai dengan karung saudara kandungnya, tipu dayanya mungkin akan terbongkar dan diketahui, jadi sebaliknya ia mulai dari saudara-saudara yang lain kemudian mencari dalam karung saudara kandungnya. Allah telah menetapkan bahwa ia akan menahan saudaranya, sehingga Dia memudahkan urusannya tanpa meninggalkan ruang untuk keraguan dari saudara-saudaranya yang lain. l Menepati janji sangat penting dalam Islam, dan seorang Muslim harus memiliki rasa tanggung jawab dan berusaha sepenuhnya untuk memenuhi apa pun yang ia janjikan. Ketika para saudara menyerah untuk membebaskan saudara mereka yang dituduh dari Yusuf, alaihissalam, mereka mengadakan musyawarah sebagaimana Allah berfirman dalam ayat kedelapan puluh yang artinya: "Maka tatkala mereka berputus asa daripadanya, mereka menyendiri sambil berunding dengan berbisik-bisik. Berkatalah yang tertua di antara mereka: Tidakkah kamu ketahui bahwa sesungguhnya ayahmu telah mengambil janji dari kamu dengan nama Allah..." [Yusuf: 80] Saudara tertua merasakan tanggung jawab dari janji yang telah mereka berikan kepada ayah mereka dan menolak untuk pergi sampai ayah mereka memberikan izin kepada mereka semua untuk kembali tanpa adik mereka, atau sampai mereka menemukan cara untuk memecahkan masalah dengan saudara yang dituduh. Hal ini mencerminkan bahwa para saudara telah berubah dari ketika mereka melempar Yusuf, alaihissalam, ke dalam sumur, dan bahwa mereka telah bertaubat kepada Allah. l Seseorang harus memberikan bukti untuk membuktikan apa yang ia klaim jika ia merasa orang-orang tidak mempercayainya, sebagaimana saudara-saudara Yusuf lakukan kepada ayah mereka, sebagaimana Allah berfirman dalam ayat kedelapan puluh dua yang artinya: "Dan tanyakanlah kepada (penduduk) negeri yang kami berada di situ, dan kafilah yang kami datang bersamanya, dan sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang benar." [Yusuf: 82] Artinya: mintalah sumber-sumber lain untuk membenarkan cerita kami. l Seseorang harus selalu berprasangka baik kepada Allah karena ini adalah salah satu implikasi dari tauhid. Meskipun Ya'qub, alaihissalam, telah berpisah dari putranya Yusuf, alaihissalam, selama lebih dari sepuluh tahun, ia berkata sebagaimana Allah berfirman dalam ayat kedelapan puluh tiga yang artinya: "Mudah-mudahan Allah mendatangkan mereka semuanya kepadaku." [Yusuf: 83] l Menangis tidak bertentangan dengan kesabaran. Allah berfirman tentang Nabi Ya'qub, alaihissalam, dalam ayat kedelapan puluh empat yang artinya: "Dan Ya'qub berpaling dari mereka (anak-anaknya) seraya berkata: Aduhai duka citaku terhadap Yusuf, dan kedua matanya menjadi putih karena kesedihan.
content_ar:
ЩӮШұЩ‘ШЁ ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ШЈШ®Ш§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩӮЩҠЩӮ ЩҲШЈЩҶШІЩ„ ШҘШ®ЩҲШӘЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШўШ®ШұЩҠЩҶ Ш§Ш«ЩҶЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШұЩҒШ©ШҢ ЩҲШ®ШөЩ‘Шө Щ„ШЈШ®ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩӮЩҠЩӮ ШәШұЩҒШ© Щ…ЩҶЩҒШұШҜШ© ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҮ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШӯЩғЩү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШўЩҠШ© Ш§Щ„ШӘШ§ШіШ№Ш© ЩҲШ§Щ„ШіШӘЩҠЩҶ: пҙҝЩӮЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩҗЩ‘ЩҠ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ ШЈЩҺШ®ЩҸЩҲЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШЁЩ’ШӘЩҺШҰЩҗШіЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩ…ЩҺШ§ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺпҙҫ [ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ: 69].

ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҒЩҲШ§ШҰШҜ: Ш§ШіШӘШ®ШҜЩ… ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙШұЩҲШ№Ш© Щ„ЩҠШЁЩӮЩҠ ШЈШ®Ш§ЩҮ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШўЩҠШӘЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӯШ§ШҜЩҠШ© ЩҲШ§Щ„ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ: пҙҝЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ‘Ш§ Ш¬ЩҺЩҮЩҺЩ‘ШІЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ… ШЁЩҗШ¬ЩҺЩҮЩҺШ§ШІЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Ш¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҗЩ‘ЩӮЩҺШ§ЩҠЩҺШ©ЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ШұЩҺШӯЩ’Щ„Щҗ ШЈЩҺШ®ЩҗЩҠЩҮЩҗ Ш«ЩҸЩ…ЩҺЩ‘ ШЈЩҺШ°ЩҺЩ‘ЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҸШӨЩҺШ°ЩҗЩ‘ЩҶЩҢ ШЈЩҺЩҠЩҺЩ‘ШӘЩҸЩҮЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҗЩҠШұЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩҺЩ‘ЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺШіЩҺШ§ШұЩҗЩӮЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺпҙҫ [ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ: 70-71]. ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҒЩҲШ§ШҰШҜ: ЩҠШ¬ЩҲШІ ШӘЩӮШ§Ш¶ЩҠ ШЈШ¬Шұ Щ…ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш«ЩҲШұ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҒЩӮЩҲШҜ ШЈЩҲ ШӘШЈШ¬ЩҠШұ ШҙШ®Шө Щ„Щ…ЩҮЩ…Ш© Щ…Ш§ШҢ Щ…Ш§ ШҜШ§Щ… Ш§Щ„ШЈШ¬Шұ Щ…ШӯШҜШҜШ§ЩӢ: пҙҝЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺЩҶ Ш¬ЩҺШ§ШЎЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ШӯЩҗЩ…Щ’Щ„ЩҸ ШЁЩҺШ№ЩҗЩҠШұЩҚпҙҫ [ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ: 72]. Ш«Щ… ШЈЩҲЩӮШ№ ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ ШҘШ®ЩҲШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ® ШӘШҜШұЩҠШ¬ЩҠШ§ЩӢ ШЁШіШӨШ§Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШўЩҠШ© Ш§Щ„ШұШ§ШЁШ№Ш© ЩҲШ§Щ„ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ: пҙҝЩӮЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ Ш¬ЩҺШІЩҺШ§ШӨЩҸЩҮЩҸпҙҫ [ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ: 74]. ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҠЩҸЩӮШұШұЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш№ЩӮЩҲШЁШ© ШЁШЈЩҶЩҒШіЩҮЩ… ЩҲЩҮЩҠ Ш§ШіШӘШ№ШЁШ§ШҜ Ш§Щ„ШіШ§ШұЩӮШҢ Щ…Щ…Ш§ ШЈШӘШ§Шӯ Щ„ЩҠЩҲШіЩҒ ШҘШЁЩӮШ§ШЎ ШЈШ®ЩҠЩҮ.
